steps.title: Identify what the colon clause proves
steps.content: After the blank, the colon introduces the evidence: cartes de visite "made it easy and enjoyable for everyday people to have their pictures taken" and "people at the time loved exchanging" them. Both clauses describe wide adoption of photography by ordinary people.

steps.title: Match a word to "spread to everyday people"
steps.content: The verb in the blank must mean "make widely accepted by ordinary people." "Popularize" fits exactly: to cause something to become liked and used by many.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'weaken': the evidence shows photography spreading and being loved, the opposite of weakening. Contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — 'praise': people enjoyed and exchanged photos, but the passage describes adoption, not verbal praise of the medium. Misreads the evidence. ✗
  • Choice C — 'popularize': easy, enjoyable, beloved by everyday people is the definition of becoming popular. ✓
  • Choice D — 'isolate': cartes de visite were exchanged with friends and family, the opposite of isolating photography. Contradicts the passage. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
